components.purpose: Compare legacy/backpressure-heavy policy against V2_18C_FIX1 controlled-courage policy on the same event surface.
Run LEGACY_POLICY and FIX1_POLICY labels side-by-side without trade, provider, wallet, or DB writes.
Quantify candidates released by FIX1 where legacy policy would have downranked or blocked.
Flag candidates where legacy/fix1 disagreement may represent false negative recovery.
Separate true opportunity recovery from unsafe false-positive recovery.
Ensure hard caps, red zone, provider degraded, and stale quote protections cannot be weakened by opportunity-cost logic.
Provider degraded remains mandatory RED; no missed-signal replay can override it.
Replay uses fixed snapshot fields only and does not perform graph searches, API fetches, sleeps, loops over external data, or AI inference.
Recovered opportunity candidates are review labels only, never execution permissions.
Prepare future memory labels for missed signal cases without writing live learning tables.
If FIX1 shows unsafe recovery, route back to V2_18C_FIX protocol instead of advancing.
Bind replay conclusions to committed V2_18C_FIX1 seal artifacts and exclude old FAIL artifacts.

conditions: Replay compares old policy and FIX1 policy on identical modeled event rows.
Opportunity recovery is not trade permission.
Hard cap and provider degraded are non-overridable.
Green/yellow recovery cannot bypass safety gates.
TempDB only; no live DB/schema/panel write.
Hot path wait count must remain zero.
Real sleep count must remain zero.
Production benchmark remains future proof requirement.
Old V2_18C failed dry-run artifacts remain excluded.
If unsafe recovered opportunity is found, return to FIX protocol.
goal: Replay legacy policy versus FIX1 policy to quantify opportunity cost recovery, missed signal candidates, avoided-loss labels, and unsafe-recovery regressions.

replay_rules.meaning: Candidate was blocked/downranked by legacy but allowed or allowed-with-friction by FIX1.
Candidate was allowed by legacy but blocked by FIX1.
Both policies backpressure without hard cap.
Provider degraded stays RED under all policies.
FIX1 recovers candidate but keeps yellow/friction label.
Recovered candidate enters future replay evidence queue only.
Blocked candidate would likely have failed under risk labels.
